Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).1: Review the Art History timeline on pp. viii and ix; read Chapter 1 of Ever Ancient, Ever New, The Principles of Design. Optional: Art Pad #1, Color Wheel Spiral.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).2: Read Unit 1, Chapter 2, Naturalism in the Italian Renaissance. Optional: Art Pad #2, Infinity Hallway with 3D Objects, Part 1.

Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).11: Read Unit 3, Chapter 11, Still Lifes of the Dutch Golden Age. Optional: Art Pad #11, Still Life Place Setting, Part 1.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).12: Read Unit 3, Chapter 12, Genre Scenes of the Dutch Golden Age. Optional: Art Pad #12, Still Life Place Setting, Part 2.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).13: Read Unit 4, Chapter 13, Peter Paul Rubens. Optional: Art Pad #13, Baroque Garland Project, Part 1.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).14: Read Unit 4, Chapter 14, Anthony van Dyck. Optional: Art Pad #14, Baroque Garland Project, Part 2.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).15: Read Unit 4, Chapter 15, Diego Velazquez. Optional: Art Pad #15, Baroque Garland Project, Part 3.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).16: Read Unit 4, Chapter 16, Bartoleme Esteban Murillo. Optional: Art Pad #16, Genre Scene.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).17: Read Unit 4, Chapter 17, French Baroque Artists. Optional: Art Pad #17, Night Sky Project, Part 1: Tints and Shades.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).18: Read Unit 4, Chapter 18, The Exuberant Style. Optional: Art Pad #18, Night Sky Project, Part 2: Extreme View Perspective.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).19: Read Unit 5, Chapter 19, The Art of Louis XIV. Optional: Art Pad #19, Two-Point Perspective: Classical Temple or Medieval Castle.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).20: Read Unit 5, Chapter 20, The Rococo. Optional: Art Pad #20, Asymmetrical Balance Project.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).21: Read Unit 5, Chapter 21, Neoclassicism. Optional: Art Pad #21, Neoclassical History Scene, Part 1.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).22: Read Unit 5, Chapter 22, Romanticism. Optional: Art Pad #22, Neoclassical History Scene, Part 2.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).23: Read Unit 5, Chapter 23, Romantic Landscapes. Optional: Art Pad #23, Romantic Landscape, Part 1: Expressive Sky.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).24: Read Unit 5, Chapter 24, The Pre-Raphaelite Brotherhood. Optional: Art Pad #24, Tessellation Pattern.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).25: Read Unit 6, Chapter 25, Realism. Optional: Art Pad #25, Romantic Landscape, Part 2: Silhouette.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).26: Read Unit 6, Chapter 26, More Realism. Optional: Art Pad #26, Draw a Horse or Tiger.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).27: Read Unit 6, Chapter 27, Impressionism. Optional: Art Pad #27, Impressionist Birch Trees.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).28: Read Unit 6, Chapter 28, Post-Impressionism. Optional: Art Pad #28, Van Gogh Cityscapes.

Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).31: Read Unit 7, Chapter 33, American Realism and Unit 7, Chapter 34, American Impressionism. Optional: Art Pad #33, Experimenting with the Rule of Thirds and Art Pad #34, Shading with Layers of Paint.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).32: Read Unit 7, Chapter 35, American Modern Art and Unit 7, Chapter 36, Conclusion. Optional: Art Pad #35, Wayne Thiebaud Candy Composition, Part 1 and Art Pad #36, Wayne Thiebaud Candy Composition, Part 2.

History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) S.1: Locate on your original outline map of Europe: Alps, Pyrenees, Italy, France (Gaul), Ireland, Germany, Poland, Hungary, Rome, Alexandria, Antioch, Constantinople, Jerusalem, New Carthage (Cartagena, Spain), Milan, Vienna, Arles, Reims, London, Canterbury, Armagh.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) S.2: Locate on your original outline map of Europe the items you entered last week. Do this from memory if possible: Alps, Pyrenees, Italy, France (Gaul), Ireland, Germany, Poland, Hungary, Rome, Alexandria, Antioch, Constantinople, Jerusalem, New Carthage (Cartagena, Spain), Milan, Vienna, Arles, Reims, London, Canterbury, Armagh.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) S.5: Take one of the photocopies of your original map and, looking, try to locate the following: Alps, Pyrenees, Italy, France (Gaul), Ireland, Germany, Poland, Hungary, Rome, Alexandria, Antioch, Constantinople, Jerusalem, New Carthage (Cartagena, Spain), Milan, Vienna, Arles, Reims, London, Canterbury, Armagh. After you have done all you can from memory, enter the rest after looking at your previous filled in map.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) S.6: Read 25 pages of Augustine Came to Kent.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) S.8: Take a test on the map study you have been doing. Take one of the photocopies of your original map and, without looking, try to locate the following: Alps, Pyrenees, Italy, France (Gaul), Ireland, Germany, Poland, Hungary, Rome, Alexandria, Antioch, Constantinople, Jerusalem, New Carthage (Cartagena, Spain), Milan, Vienna, Arles, Reims, London, Canterbury, Armagh.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) S.9: Read 25 pages of Son of Charlemagne.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) S.10: Geography: Review the Geography Land and Water Forms Definitions in the back of your atlas. You may cut these out to use over the next few weeks, make your own flashcards, or find them on Quizlet under MODG 8th Grade Geography Set 1A – Land and Water Forms (https://quizlet.com/107971609/modg-8th-grade-geography-terms-flash-cards/). Then complete Chapter 3 (Map Division), Sections 1 (Hemispheres and World Research) and 2 (Latitude and Longitude) in Eighth Grade Geography. OR If you have The Ultimate Geography and Timeline Guide: Go over the Geography Definitions Flashcards found on p. 263-280. Make a note of any terms you don’t know.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) S.11: Go over the ‘Geography Flashcards’ again. How did you do?
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) S.12: Geography: Complete Chapter 3, Sections 3 (The Tropics), and 4 (World Research Continued and Map Reading Practice) in Eighth Grade Geography. Then spend 10 minutes on the land and water form definitions or flashcards. OR If you have The Ultimate Geography and Timeline Guide: Look at the section titled “Good Stuff to Know by Heart” on p. 281-283. Ask someone in your family to ask you about each section. What do you already know? Write down in a reference notebook any of the information that you don’t know already.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) S.13: Read the King Arthur book you chose.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) S.14: Read the King Arthur book you chose.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) S.15: Geography: Complete Chapter 4 (North America), Section 1 (The Continent) in Eighth Grade Geography. OR If you have The Ultimate Geography and Timeline Guide: Turn to p. 160. Read the first half of the page. Then, using a photocopy of the map on p. 231, identify the places listed in the ‘Map-It’ section of p.160.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) S.17: Geography: Complete Chapter 4, Section 3 (Canada) in Eighth Grade Geography. OR If you have The Ultimate Geography and Timeline Guide: Turn to p. 163. Read this page and the next. Then, using a photocopy of the map on p. 234, identify the places listed in the ‘Map-It’ section of p.164.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) S.21: Geography: Complete Chapter 5, Section 2 (Brazil) and Section 3 (Keyhole Maps of South America) in Eighth Grade Geography. OR If you have The Ultimate Geography and Timeline Guide: Read pp.171-172. Then, using a photocopy of the map on p. 233, identify the items listed under ‘Map-It’ on p.172.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) S.22: Finish Francis and Clare, Saints of Assisi.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) S.23: Read St. Dominic and the Rosary.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) S.24: If you are using The Story of the Church read Part II, Unit 5, Chapters IV & V on pp. 256-269 in The Story of the Church. Page 270 contains a section titled “Experimental Science – St. Thomas Aquinas”. Answer the questions in this section, first in a discussion with your teacher, and then in writing. If you are not using The Story of the Church, read the resource in your syllabus resources titled St. Thomas Aquinas (here). There are questions after the selection. The questions are titled “Experimental Science – St. Thomas Aquinas” Answer the questions in this section, first in a discussion with your teacher and then in writing. Also, this would be a good time to read the story of St. Albert the Great and St Thomas Aquinas in The Young People’s Book of Saints.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) S.25: Read The Door in the Wall.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) S.26: Read St. Elizabeth of Hungary, St. Willebold, and St. Louis IX in The Young People’s Book of Saints if you are using that text. Also you would like one of the following supplemental books if you have them: The Blue Gonfalon by Margaret Hubbard (in print), Where Valor Lies by Adele and Cateau De Leeuw (in print), The Two Trumpeters of Vienna by Hertha Pauli (out of print), or St. Louis and the Last Crusade by Margaret Hubbard (out of print).
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) S.27: Geography: Complete Chapter 8 (Africa) in Eighth Grade Geography. OR If you have The Ultimate Geography and Timeline Guide: Read pp.188-190. Then, using a photocopy of the map on p. 220, identify the items listed under ‘Map-It’ on p.190.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) S.30: Finish Boy Knight of Reims. (In the Days of King Hal by Marion Taggert (out of print) and The Life of St. Joan by Louis de Wohl (in print) would be nice to read here as well. These are two good books.)
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) S.31: If you are using The Story of the Church read Part III, Unit 1, Chapter IV on pp. 311- 319 in The Story of the Church. If you are using The Young People’s Book of Saints read about St. Vincent de Paul, St. Alphonsus Ligori, St. Benedict Joseph Labre, and The Cure D’Ars.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) S.32: Now, write a summary of your history class this year. (You would enjoy reading The Trumpeter of Krakow by Eric Kelly, The Blood Red Crescent by Henry Garnett, and Red Hugh, Prince of Donegal by Robert T Reilly, all of which are in print.)

Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.1: ). Some of the PDF files found here are taken from the printed copy of the corresponding syllabus, if there is one. There are also resources specific to the particular course that will be assigned or referenced within the syllabus assignments. Answer Keys or Assignment overviews for the syllabus, if applicable, are only found via the parent log-in; the student log-in may access all other syllabus resources.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.2: Listen to Vivaldi’s “Four Seasons: Spring”. You could certainly listen to the whole work, all four sections, but it would be considerably longer.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.3: Have someone in your house play a minute section from the Canon in D and the Four Seasons; can you tell which one it is? Try this exercise several times.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.4: Listen to one of the Brandenburg Concertos.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.5: Have someone in your house play a minute section from the Four Seasons and the Brandenburg Concertos; can you tell which one it is? Try this exercise several times. Is it easier or harder to distinguish the Brandenburg Concerto from the Four Seasons than it was to distinguish the Concerto from the Canon in D?
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.6: Listen to the Tocatta and Fugue in D Minor.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.7: Have someone in your house play a minute section from the Tocatta and Fugue and from the Canon in D; can you tell which is which? Try this exercise several times. Is it easier or harder to distinguish between these two than it was to distinguish between the Brandenburg Concerto and the Four Seasons?
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.8: Now listen to the Water Music by Handel.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.9: Have someone play a short selection from the Water Music and a short selection from the Four Seasons; can you tell which is which? How about trying to distinguish between the Brandenburg Concerto and the Water Music? Can you do it? How about between the Water Music and the Canon in D? Which are the most alike and which the most different?
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.10: Listen to The Well Tempered Clavier by Bach.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.11: Listen to Piano Sonata no. 8 in C Minor by Beethoven.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.12: Have someone play a short selection from each of the last two pieces. Can you distinguish between them? Listen to a short selection from The Well Tempered Clavier and then from the Tocatta and Fugue in D minor. Can you distinguish between them? Which of these three pieces (Tocatta and Fugue, The Well Tempered Clavier, and the Piano Sonata no. 8) are most alike? Why?
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.13: Listen to the Oboe Concerto by Bach.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.14: Have someone play a short selection from each of the four Bach pieces we have listened to (Brandenburg Concerto, Tocatta and Fugue in D Minor, Oboe Concerto, and The Well Tempered Clavier). Let him choose the order, just calling each by a number. You try to put a name to each number. Can you do it?
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.15: Listen to the Christmas parts of the Messiah. If you have the libretto, it is easy to find the right parts. Otherwise, just listen to the words to tell where you are in Christ’s life. The words are in English, so you can tell when the music moves past the nativity. The Hallelujah chorus, with which nearly everyone is familiar, comes at the end of the work, after the passion and crucifixion, so we’ll save that for Easter.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.16: Listen to the String Quartet in C by Haydn.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.17: Listen to the Clarinet Quintet by Mozart.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.18: Have someone in your house play a three minute section from one of the last two pieces you have listened to; can you tell which one it is? Try this exercise several times.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.19: Have someone in your house play a short section from The Four Seasons and then from the Haydn string quartet; can you tell which one it is? Try this exercise several times.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.20: Listen to the Arrival of the Queen of Sheba by Handel.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.21: Have someone in your house play a short section from the Arrival of the Queen of Sheba and then from the Water Music; can you tell which is which? Then try to distinguish among the two just mentioned and the Haydn string quartet. Try this exercise several times.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.22: Listen to the Eine Kleine Nacht Music by Mozart.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.23: Listen to the Piano Sonata in C, K.545, by Mozart.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.24: Listen to the Clarinet Concerto in A Major by Mozart.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.25: Have someone play a short selection from each of the Mozart pieces we have listened to (Clarinet Quintet, Clarinet Concerto, Eine Kleine Nacht Music, Piano Sonata in C, K.545). Let him choose the order, just calling each by a number. You try to put a name to each number. Can you do it?
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.26: Listen to the Piano Sonata no. 8 in C Minor by Beethoven.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.27: Try to distinguish between Mozart’s Piano Sonata in C and Beethoven’s Piano Sonata no. 8 in C Minor. Can you do it? How would you characterize the difference?
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.29: Listen to Mozart’s Piano Concerto no. 23 in A Major.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.30: Now have someone play parts of the three piano pieces you have been listening to (Piano Sonata in C, K.545, Piano Concerto no. 23 in A Major, and Piano Sonata no. 8 in C Minor). Can you identify which is which? This is harder to do than in many of the previous exercises because the principle instrument is the same in all three pieces.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.31: This week have someone play the pieces from this year, in a random order, and try to identify as many of the pieces from the year as you can. Listen as long as you need to in order to identify the piece. Use the list from the front of this syllabus to help you.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-19.32: Try again to identify as many of the pieces from the year as you can. This time don’t use a list and see how you do.

Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).1: Review Art History timeline on pp. viii and ix; read Chapter 1 of Ever Ancient, Ever New, The Principles of Design. Optional: Art Pad #1, Color Wheel Spiral.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).2: Read Unit 1, Chapter 2, Naturalism in the Italian Renaissance. Optional: Art Pad #2, Infinity Hallway with 3D Objects, Part 1.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).3: Read Unit 1, Chapter 3, Idealism in the Italian Renaissance. Optional: Art Pad #3, Infinity Hallway with 3D Objects, Part 2.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).4: Read Unit 2, Chapter 4, The Northern Renaissance. Optional: Art Pad #4, Crosshatching a Conch Shell.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).5: Read Unit 2, Chapter 5, The Protestant Reformation. Optional: Art Pad #5, Contour Drawings of Your Hand.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).6: Read Unit 2, Chapter 6, Mannerism in Venice and Spain. Optional: Art Pad #6, Grid Drawing.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).7: Read Unit 2, Chapter 7, Mannerism in Central Italy and the Council of Trent. Optional: Art Pad #7, Divine Mercy Project, Part 1.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).8: Read Unit 2, Chapter 8, Early Baroque Art. Optional: Art Pad #8, Divine Mercy Project, Part 2.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).9: Read Unit 3, Chapter 9, Portraits and Bible Scenes of the Dutch Golden Age. Optional: Art Pad #9, Facial Portrait.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).10: Read Unit 3, Chapter 10, Landscapes of the Dutch Golden Age. Optional: Art Pad #10, Unified Landscape Composition.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).11: Read Unit 3, Chapter 11, Still Lifes of the Dutch Golden Age. Optional: Art Pad #11, Still Life Place Setting, Part 1.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).12: Read Unit 3, Chapter 12, Genre Scenes of the Dutch Golden Age. Optional: Art Pad #12, Still Life Place Setting, Part 2.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).13: Read Unit 4, Chapter 13, Peter Paul Rubens. Optional: Art Pad #13, Baroque Garland Project, Part 1.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).14: Read Unit 4, Chapter 14, Anthony van Dyck. Optional: Art Pad #14, Baroque Garland Project, Part 2.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).15: Read Unit 4, Chapter 15, Diego Velazquez. Optional: Art Pad #15, Baroque Garland Project, Part 3.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).16: Read Unit 4, Chapter 16, Bartoleme Esteban Murillo. Optional: Art Pad #16, Genre Scene.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).17: Read Unit 4, Chapter 17, French Baroque Artists. Optional: Art Pad #17, Night Sky Project, Part 1: Tints and Shades.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).18: Read Unit 4, Chapter 18, The Exuberant Style. Optional: Art Pad #18, Night Sky Project, Part 2: Extreme View Perspective.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).19: Read Unit 5, Chapter 19, The Art of Louis XIV. Optional: Art Pad #19, Two-Point Perspective: Classical Temple or Medieval Castle.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).20: Read Unit 5, Chapter 20, The Rococo. Optional: Art Pad #20, Asymmetrical Balance Project.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).21: Read Unit 5, Chapter 21, Neoclassicism. Optional: Art Pad #21, Neoclassical History Scene, Part 1.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).22: Read Unit 5, Chapter 22, Romanticism. Optional: Art Pad #22, Neoclassical History Scene, Part 2.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).23: Read Unit 5, Chapter 23, Romantic Landscapes. Optional: Art Pad #23, Romantic Landscape, Part 1: Expressive Sky.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).24: Read Unit 5, Chapter 24, The Pre-Raphaelite Brotherhood. Optional: Art Pad #24, Tessellation Pattern.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).25: Read Unit 6, Chapter 25, Realism. Optional: Art Pad #25, Romantic Landscape, Part 2: Silhouette.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).26: Read Unit 6, Chapter 26, More Realism. Optional: Art Pad #26, Draw a Horse or Tiger.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).27: Read Unit 6, Chapter 27, Impressionism. Optional: Art Pad #27, Impressionist Birch Trees.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).28: Read Unit 6, Chapter 28, Post-Impressionism. Optional: Art Pad #28, Van Gogh Cityscapes.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).29: Read Unit 6, Chapter 29, Modern Art and Unit 6, Chapter 30, Antoni Gaudi’s Sagrada Familia. Optional: Art Pad #29, Kandinsky Landscape, Part 1 and Art Pad #30, Kandinsky Landscape, Part 2.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).30: Read Unit 7, Chapter 31, American Neoclassicism and Unit 7, Chapter 32, American Romanticism. Optional: Art Pad #31, Symbolism Still Life and Art Pad #32, Flip Book Animation.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).31: Read Unit 7, Chapter 33, American Realism and Unit 7, Chapter 34, American Impressionism. Optional: Art Pad #33, Experimenting with the Rule of Thirds and Art Pad #34, Shading with Layers of Paint.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2).32: Read Unit 7, Chapter 35, American Modern Art and Unit 7, Chapter 36, Conclusion. Optional: Art Pad #35, Wayne Thiebaud Candy Composition, Part 1 and Art Pad #36, Wayne Thiebaud Candy Composition, Part 2.

Phonics & Reading K.1: Review letter sounds A–E; read short CVC words (cat, bed, pig).
Phonics & Reading K.2: Review letter sounds F–J; practice blending three-letter words.
Phonics & Reading K.3: Review letter sounds K–O; read a short pattern book together.
Phonics & Reading K.4: Review letter sounds P–T; introduce sight words 'the' and 'and'.
Phonics & Reading K.5: Review letter sounds U–Z; practice sight words 'I', 'a', 'see'.
Phonics & Reading K.6: Read short CVC words with short-A; identify rhyming pairs.
Phonics & Reading K.7: Read short CVC words with short-E; copy two sight words neatly.
Phonics & Reading K.8: Read short CVC words with short-I; reread a familiar pattern book.
Phonics & Reading K.9: Read short CVC words with short-O; introduce sight word 'is'.
Phonics & Reading K.10: Read short CVC words with short-U; review all five short vowels.
Phonics & Reading K.15: Mixed digraph review; read a short story aloud.
Phonics & Reading K.16: Long vowel A (silent-e): cake, lake, gate.
Phonics & Reading K.17: Long vowel I (silent-e): bike, ride, kite.
Phonics & Reading K.18: Long vowel O (silent-e): bone, home, rope.
Phonics & Reading K.19: Long vowel U (silent-e): cube, tube, mule.
Phonics & Reading K.20: Mixed long-vowel review; read a short Bob Book.
Phonics & Reading K.21: Beginning blends bl, br: blue, brown.
Phonics & Reading K.22: Beginning blends cl, cr: clap, crab.
Phonics & Reading K.23: Beginning blends fl, fr: flag, frog.
Phonics & Reading K.24: Beginning blends gl, gr, pl, pr: glad, grin, plan, prim.
Phonics & Reading K.25: Beginning blends sl, sm, sn, sp, st, sw: slip, smell, snap.
Phonics & Reading K.30: R-controlled vowels ar, or: car, fork.
Phonics & Reading K.31: Read a level-A or B early reader independently.
Phonics & Reading K.32: End-of-year review: read aloud and retell a short story.

Math K (Saxon Math K).1: Count to 20 forward; identify written numerals 0–10.
Math K (Saxon Math K).2: Count to 30; sort objects by color.
Math K (Saxon Math K).3: Count to 40; identify shapes: circle, square, triangle.
Math K (Saxon Math K).4: Count to 50; copy a simple AB pattern with manipulatives.
Math K (Saxon Math K).5: Count by 1s to 60; compare two groups (more, fewer, same).
Math K (Saxon Math K).6: Count by 1s to 70; introduce written numerals 11–15.
Math K (Saxon Math K).7: Count by 1s to 80; introduce written numerals 16–20.
Math K (Saxon Math K).8: Count by 10s to 100; practice writing numerals 0–9.
Math K (Saxon Math K).10: Introduce addition concept with manipulatives (sums to 5).
Math K (Saxon Math K).11: Addition facts to 5 with counters; write the equations.
Math K (Saxon Math K).12: Addition facts to 10 with counters; oral practice.
Math K (Saxon Math K).13: Days of the week and months of the year orally.
Math K (Saxon Math K).14: Tell time to the hour on an analog clock.
Math K (Saxon Math K).15: Identify pennies, nickels, dimes; count by 1s and 5s.
Math K (Saxon Math K).16: Compare lengths: longer / shorter / about the same.
Math K (Saxon Math K).17: Compare weights: heavier / lighter using a balance.
Math K (Saxon Math K).25: Introduce greater than / less than with two numbers under 10.
Math K (Saxon Math K).26: Identify halves and quarters with paper folding.
Math K (Saxon Math K).27: Read a simple bar graph with 3 categories.
Math K (Saxon Math K).28: Word problems: combine two small groups (sums to 10).
Math K (Saxon Math K).29: Word problems: take away from a small group.
Math K (Saxon Math K).30: Money: count combinations of pennies and nickels to 25¢.
Math K (Saxon Math K).31: Time: identify the half hour on an analog clock.
Math K (Saxon Math K).32: End-of-year review: count to 100, write 0–20, simple sums.

Religion K (Faith and Life K).1: God made everything — discuss creation with picture book.
Religion K (Faith and Life K).2: God made me — child draws a self-portrait and names a gift God gave him.
Religion K (Faith and Life K).3: Make the Sign of the Cross slowly together; practice three times.
Religion K (Faith and Life K).4: Memorize: 'Glory be to the Father' (first half).
Religion K (Faith and Life K).5: Memorize: 'Glory be to the Father' (second half); recite whole prayer.
Religion K (Faith and Life K).6: Story of Adam and Eve in the garden — read and discuss.
Religion K (Faith and Life K).8: Memorize: 'Hail Mary' (second half); recite whole prayer.
Religion K (Faith and Life K).9: Story of Noah's Ark — read and discuss God's promise.
Religion K (Faith and Life K).10: Memorize: 'Our Father' (first phrase: 'Our Father, who art in heaven, hallowed be thy name').
Religion K (Faith and Life K).11: Memorize: 'Our Father' (next phrase: 'Thy kingdom come, thy will be done').
Religion K (Faith and Life K).12: Memorize: 'Our Father' (next phrase: 'Give us this day our daily bread').
Religion K (Faith and Life K).13: Memorize: 'Our Father' (final phrase); recite whole prayer.
Religion K (Faith and Life K).14: Story of Abraham — God's friend who trusted Him.
Religion K (Faith and Life K).15: Story of Moses and the burning bush.
Religion K (Faith and Life K).16: The Ten Commandments — name the first three with Mom.
Religion K (Faith and Life K).19: Advent / Christmas: the Annunciation — read Luke 1 simply.
Religion K (Faith and Life K).20: Christmas: the Nativity — talk about the manger.
Religion K (Faith and Life K).22: The Holy Family — Joseph, Mary, and Jesus.
Religion K (Faith and Life K).23: Jesus is baptized by John in the Jordan.
Religion K (Faith and Life K).24: Jesus calls the first apostles; name a few.
Religion K (Faith and Life K).25: Jesus heals the sick — pick one Gospel story to read.
Religion K (Faith and Life K).27: Lent: Jesus prays and fasts in the desert 40 days.
Religion K (Faith and Life K).28: Holy Week: Palm Sunday — read briefly.
Religion K (Faith and Life K).30: Mary, our Mother — pray a decade of the Rosary together.
Religion K (Faith and Life K).31: Saints: pick one favorite saint and tell his story.
Religion K (Faith and Life K).32: End-of-year review: recite Sign of the Cross, Glory Be, Hail Mary, Our Father.

Handwriting K (Handwriting Without Tears.1: Practice letters L and F (vertical and horizontal lines).
Handwriting K (Handwriting Without Tears.14: Review all uppercase letters; write own first name.
Handwriting K (Handwriting Without Tears.19: Practice lowercase l, k, y, j.
Handwriting K (Handwriting Without Tears.20: Practice lowercase p, r, n, m.
Handwriting K (Handwriting Without Tears.23: Write own first and last name.
Handwriting K (Handwriting Without Tears.24: Copy three short words: cat, dog, sun.
Handwriting K (Handwriting Without Tears.25: Copy a short sentence: 'I see a cat.'
Handwriting K (Handwriting Without Tears.26: Copy a short sentence: 'The sun is up.'
Handwriting K (Handwriting Without Tears.27: Copy a short sentence: 'God is good.'
Handwriting K (Handwriting Without Tears.30: Write the days of the week (with help).
Handwriting K (Handwriting Without Tears.32: End-of-year review: write own name, address, and one short sentence.

Art K.1: Crayon free draw — name three colors used.
Art K.2: Color in a picture of a saint; stay inside the lines.
Art K.3: Cut and paste shapes to make a house.
Art K.4: Finger paint — explore primary colors red, yellow, blue.
Art K.5: Mix red + yellow → orange; paint a pumpkin.
Art K.6: Mix blue + yellow → green; paint grass and a tree.
Art K.7: Mix red + blue → purple; paint flowers.
Art K.8: Draw your family — label each person.
Art K.9: Draw your home — include a door, two windows, a roof.
Art K.10: Watercolor wash — practice loading the brush with water.
Art K.12: Make a leaf rubbing with crayon.
Art K.13: Trace your own hand and decorate it.
Art K.14: Cut a paper snowflake from a folded square.
Art K.15: Make a Christmas card for a grandparent.
Art K.17: Make a paper crown for the Wise Men.
Art K.18: Tear-paper collage of a winter tree.
Art K.19: Draw and color a winter snow scene.
Art K.20: Modeling clay — make a coiled snake.
Art K.21: Modeling clay — make a small bowl by pinching.
Art K.22: Draw a self-portrait with details (eyes, nose, mouth, hair).
Art K.24: Make an Easter card for Mom or Dad.
Art K.26: Draw your favorite Bible story scene.
Art K.27: Print with sliced fruits or vegetables.
Art K.30: Outdoor sketch — draw something you see in the yard.
Art K.32: End-of-year review: pick a favorite project and tell Mom about it.
